intro: Alpinia officinarum, also known as lesser galangal or balm, is a type of rhizomatous perennial plant native to the tropical regions of Asia. Its underground rhizomes contain oils with aromatic properties and are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, indigestion, and respiratory issues. In addition to its medicinal uses, alpinia officinarum balm is also used in the culinary world to add flavor to dishes, particularly in Southeast Asian cuisine.

fever: Alpinia officinarum balm, also known as Ginger Balm, has been traditionally used to help alleviate fever and reduce body temperature due to its warming and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, Alpinia officinarum balm may provide fast relief from fever, making it a popular remedy among herbalists and natural health enthusiasts.
recipe: To make alpinia officinarum balm for medicinal use, combine equal parts of distilled water and witch hazel in a bowl, then add 10-15 drops of alpinia officinarum essential oil, which is extracted from the rhizome of the plant. Stir the mixture well and transfer it to a small glass bottle. Store the balm in the refrigerator to keep it fresh, and apply a small amount topically as needed, ideally after consulting with a healthcare professional for proper dosage and usage.
